problem.statement: The IDE projection line needs one tighter rollout program because the current child specs still span too much scope for direct implementation.
problem.scope: Program-level rollout planning for the phase-1 and phase-2 contract set.
problem.symptom: Broad child specs still assess as multi-spec work instead of implementation-sized specs.

business_rule: each child spec owns one bounded contract
execution_flow: define child spec order and dependency

hypotheses.statement: A tighter rollout portfolio will let the actual implementation start without reopening broad planning every round.

risks.statement: A child spec may start absorbing adjacent concerns again if dependency edges are not explicit.

decision_execution_path.decision: split bounded child specs by contract

triggers: a child spec grows into mixed onboarding scope
phase-2 work blocks the phase-1 gate
actions: move the concern to the correct child spec
attach debug evidence when a child spec boundary is violated
